title: Chouchou Refuses to Abandon the Shop
description: On pages 1–3, Chouchou’s loyalty to the pet food shop turns Buggy’s occupation from a pirate threat into a personal wound for the town.
scene: Chouchou refuses to give up the place connected to his owner, while pirates and bystanders react to the dog’s stubborn defense.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I've got to tell him that the kid is a rubber man...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: THE KID!? THE KID IN THE STRAW HAT BEAT YOU!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: MOHJI was trying to tell us some- thing.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: HE COULD BARELY STAND! MUST HAVE BEEN IMPORTANT...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: What kind of rub could do that to a man!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Hmm... that boy must have used some diabolical rubbing technique on him...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: AR! THE SKIP- PER'S GOT IT!

dialogue.speaker: Mohji
dialogue.text: Beaten by a Masser!! If this gets out, our repu- tattoo's scuttled!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: load the Buggy balls, you swabs!! We've known to destroy!!

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Boodle
dialogue.text: The refuge of the towns- people

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Hey! It's chouchou from the pet food shop!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: WE'RE GLAD YOU'RE OKAY, BOY! WE WERE WORRIED ABOUT YOU.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Let's take care of his wounds.

summary: This part makes the conflict emotional: Luffy sees that Buggy’s violence is not abstract, but aimed at things people love and protect.
